tasks.description:
Verify the entire Phase 1 deliverable before moving to Phase 2. Run all type and test checks, then commit Phase 1 atomically.

Commands to run (in order):

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/shared
pnpm lint apps/client/src/layers/shared
```

All three must be green. If any fail, fix the issue in the originating task before proceeding.

Verification checklist:
- [ ] `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/use-dialog-tab-state.ts` exists
- [ ] `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/__tests__/use-dialog-tab-state.test.ts` exists, all 6 tests pass

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P2] Convert PreferencesTab to SwitchSettingRow
tasks.description:
Convert all `<SettingRow>+<Switch>` instances in `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/tabs/PreferencesTab.tsx` to `<SwitchSettingRow>`. The current file has 9 instances (the spec table says 8 but the actual count is 9 — verify and convert all).

**Before/After example:**

```tsx
// Before
<SettingRow label="Show timestamps" description="Display message timestamps in chat">
  <Switch checked={showTimestamps} onCheckedChange={setShowTimestamps} />
</SettingRow>

// After
<SwitchSettingRow
  label="Show timestamps"
  description="Display message timestamps in chat"
  checked={showTimestamps}
  onCheckedChange={setShowTimestamps}
/>
```

**Imports to update:**

Replace:
```tsx
import {
  Switch,
  SettingRow,
  FieldCard,
  FieldCardContent,
  NavigationLayoutPanelHeader,
} from '@/layers/shared/ui';
```

With:
```tsx
import {
  SwitchSettingRow,
  FieldCard,
  FieldCardContent,
  NavigationLayoutPanelHeader,
} from '@/layers/shared/ui';
```

(Drop both `Switch` and `SettingRow` since neither is used after conversion.)

**Each instance to convert** (verify against the actual file before editing):
1. Show timestamps → showTimestamps / setShowTimestamps
2. Expand tool calls → expandToolCalls / setExpandToolCalls
3. Auto-hide tool calls → autoHideToolCalls / setAutoHideToolCalls
4. Show shortcut chips → showShortcutChips / setShowShortcutChips
5. Task celebrations → showTaskCelebrations / setShowTaskCelebrations
6. Notification sound → enableNotificationSound / setEnableNotificationSound
7. Tasks run notifications → enableTasksNotifications / setEnableTasksNotifications
8. Feature suggestions → promoEnabled / setPromoEnabled
9. Show dev tools → devtoolsOpen / `() => toggleDevtools()` (special — wrap toggle in a lambda since `toggleDevtools` takes no args; pass `(checked) => toggleDevtools()`)

Acceptance Criteria:
- Zero `<SettingRow>` and zero `<Switch>` JSX usages remain in `PreferencesTab.tsx`
- All 9 toggles still function (manual smoke test in Phase 2 verification)
- File still under 300 lines
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings` is green

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P2] Convert AdvancedTab toggle rows to SwitchSettingRow
tasks.description:
Convert the 2 simple `<SettingRow>+<Switch>` instances in `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/AdvancedTab.tsx` to `<SwitchSettingRow>`. Do NOT touch the structural changes for AdvancedTab — those are handled in Phase 3 (task 3.3). This task ONLY handles the row-level conversion.

**Instances to convert:**

```tsx
// Before
<SettingRow
  label="Multi-window sync"
  description="Keep multiple DorkOS windows and the Obsidian plugin in sync"
>
  <Switch checked={enableCrossClientSync} onCheckedChange={setEnableCrossClientSync} />
</SettingRow>

// After
<SwitchSettingRow
  label="Multi-window sync"
  description="Keep multiple DorkOS windows and the Obsidian plugin in sync"
  checked={enableCrossClientSync}
  onCheckedChange={setEnableCrossClientSync}
/>
```

And:

```tsx
// Before
<SettingRow
  label="Background refresh"
  description="Check for new messages periodically, even when no one is responding"
>
  <Switch checked={enableMessagePolling} onCheckedChange={setEnableMessagePolling} />
</SettingRow>

// After
<SwitchSettingRow
  label="Background refresh"
  description="Check for new messages periodically, even when no one is responding"
  checked={enableMessagePolling}
  onCheckedChange={setEnableMessagePolling}
/>
```

Do NOT convert the `SettingRow` instances that wrap a `Select`, `Input`, or `LogLocationRow` — those are not toggles. Specifically: `Log level` (Select), `Max log file size` (Input), `Rotated files kept` (Input), `Log location` (custom button) — these all stay as `SettingRow`.

**Imports**: keep `SettingRow` import (still used for non-Switch rows), add `SwitchSettingRow`, drop `Switch` (no longer used in this file).

Acceptance Criteria:
- The 2 toggle rows now use `SwitchSettingRow`
- The non-toggle `SettingRow` instances remain unchanged
- No `Switch` import left in the file
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- AdvancedTab tests still pass (this task does not change AdvancedTab's prop signature — that happens in 3.3)

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P2] Audit AgentsTab for SwitchSettingRow conversions
tasks.description:
Audit `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/AgentsTab.tsx` for `<SettingRow>+<Switch>` patterns. Per pre-investigation, this file currently contains 0 simple toggle rows — it has `SettingRow` paired with `Select` (Default agent) and `Input` (Max concurrent sessions, Default timeout) and a custom display row for the Claude CLI path.

Action:
1. Confirm the audit by reading the current file and grepping for `<Switch` inside `<SettingRow>`
2. If you find any toggle rows, convert them per the same pattern as 2.1 / 2.2
3. If you find NONE, document the audit result in the task close-out and move on (no code changes)

Per the spec's table in §6.9, this entry says "0–1 (verify)" — so a zero result is fully expected.

Acceptance Criteria:
- File audited and result documented ("0 conversions needed" or "N conversions made")
- If conversions were needed, all `<Switch>` JSX usages inside `<SettingRow>` are eliminated
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/AgentsTab.test.tsx` green

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P2] Audit agent-settings ContextTab/ToolsTab for conversions
tasks.description:
Audit the agent-settings tab files for convertible `<SettingRow>+<Switch>` patterns. Note: there is NO `CapabilitiesTab.tsx` in the agent-settings module — the spec lists this as TBD/audit. Pre-investigation found:

- `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/ContextTab.tsx` (line 84-98): uses `<SettingRow>` with a `<Switch>` plus a conditional `<Badge>` and `disabled={!available}` and `aria-label`. The Switch and Badge live inside a `<div className="flex items-center gap-2">`. **Decision:** SKIP this conversion — the row contains a Badge alongside the Switch, which `SwitchSettingRow` does not support without losing functionality. Document the skip reason and move on.
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx`: rows at lines 131, 151, 338, 358 — all are complex (Tooltip + Badge + ToolCountBadge + Switch + Reset button). **Decision:** SKIP all — these are complex compound rows that match the same pattern as `ToolGroupRow` (which the spec explicitly excludes in §6.9).
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/PersonalityTab.tsx`: line 205 row uses `<SettingRow>` with a `<Select>` (not a Switch) — no conversion applies.

Action:
1. Re-verify the above by reading each file
2. If any of the SKIP decisions are wrong (i.e., a clean `<SettingRow>+<Switch>` toggle exists), convert it per the standard pattern
3. Otherwise, document "0 conversions in agent-settings — all SettingRow+Switch instances are complex compound rows" and move on

Acceptance Criteria:
- Audit complete and documented in the task close-out
- Any pure `<SettingRow>+<Switch>` toggles converted (likely 0)
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings` green
tasks.activeForm: Auditing agent-settings tabs for toggle conversions

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P2] Convert RateLimitSection rate-limit toggle
tasks.description:
Convert the rate-limit toggle in `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/external-mcp/RateLimitSection.tsx` to `<SwitchSettingRow>`.

**Before:**

```tsx
<SettingRow label="Rate limiting" description="Limit external MCP requests per time window">
  <Switch
    checked={rateLimit.enabled}
    onCheckedChange={(v) => onUpdate({ enabled: v })}
    aria-label="Toggle rate limiting"
  />
</SettingRow>
```

**After:**

```tsx
<SwitchSettingRow
  label="Rate limiting"
  description="Limit external MCP requests per time window"
  checked={rateLimit.enabled}
  onCheckedChange={(v) => onUpdate({ enabled: v })}
  ariaLabel="Toggle rate limiting"
/>
```

Note the prop rename: `aria-label` (HTML attribute on `Switch`) becomes `ariaLabel` (camelCase prop on `SwitchSettingRow`).

**Imports**: replace `Input, SettingRow, Switch` with `Input, SwitchSettingRow` in the import from `@/layers/shared/ui`.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The rate-limit toggle uses `SwitchSettingRow`
- `aria-label` text "Toggle rate limiting" preserved via `ariaLabel` prop
- The `Input` rows below the toggle are unchanged (they're not toggles)
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/ExternalMcpCard.test.tsx` green

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P3] Make ServerTab self-contained
[settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P3] Add restartOverlayOpen to app-store panels slice
tasks.description:
Add `restartOverlayOpen` boolean state and `setRestartOverlayOpen` setter to the panels slice at `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store/app-store-panels.ts`. This lifts the local state currently in `SettingsDialog.tsx` (line 43: `const [restartOverlayOpen, setRestartOverlayOpen] = useState(false);`) into the global app store so `AdvancedTab` can dispatch directly without passing callbacks through `SettingsDialog`.

**Edits to `app-store-panels.ts`:**

1. Add fields to the `PanelsSlice` interface (insert near the other dialog open-state fields, e.g., after `meshOpen`):

```ts
/** Server restart overlay — opened by AdvancedTab after Reset/Restart actions. */
restartOverlayOpen: boolean;
setRestartOverlayOpen: (open: boolean) => void;
```

2. Add the field implementations to `createPanelsSlice` (insert in the same relative location):

```ts
restartOverlayOpen: false,
setRestartOverlayOpen: (open) => set({ restartOverlayOpen: open }),
```

The pattern matches existing fields in the slice (e.g., `relayOpen` / `setRelayOpen`). No persistence — the panels slice is transient by design (per the slice's TSDoc).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Both interface members added
- Both implementation members added
- File still under 300 lines
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/shared/model` (if any tests exist) green

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P3] Refactor AdvancedTab to dispatch to store
tasks.description:
Refactor `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/AdvancedTab.tsx` to remove the `onResetComplete` and `onRestartComplete` callback props. Replace them with a direct dispatch to `useAppStore((s) => s.setRestartOverlayOpen)` (added in task 3.2).

**Changes:**

1. Remove the `AdvancedTabProps` interface entirely.
2. Change the component signature from `export function AdvancedTab({ onResetComplete, onRestartComplete }: AdvancedTabProps)` to `export function AdvancedTab()`.
3. Add a store selector inside the component:

```tsx
const setRestartOverlayOpen = useAppStore((s) => s.setRestartOverlayOpen);
```

4. Replace the `<ResetDialog ... onResetComplete={onResetComplete} />` with `<ResetDialog ... onResetComplete={() => setRestartOverlayOpen(true)} />`.
5. Replace the `<RestartDialog ... onRestartComplete={onRestartComplete} />` with `<RestartDialog ... onRestartComplete={() => setRestartOverlayOpen(true)} />`.

**Resulting tail of the file:**

```tsx
      <ResetDialog
        open={resetDialogOpen}
        onOpenChange={setResetDialogOpen}
        onResetComplete={() => setRestartOverlayOpen(true)}
      />
      <RestartDialog
        open={restartDialogOpen}
        onOpenChange={setRestartDialogOpen}
        onRestartComplete={() => setRestartOverlayOpen(true)}
      />
```

Acceptance Criteria:
- `AdvancedTab` is parameterless (no props interface)
- Store selector for `setRestartOverlayOpen` added
- `ResetDialog` and `RestartDialog` callbacks dispatch to the store
- The 2 `<SwitchSettingRow>` conversions from task 2.2 are still in place
- `pnpm typecheck` passes (NOTE: `SettingsDialog.tsx` still passes the old props at this point and will fail to compile — this is expected and fixed by task 4.1)
- Update `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/AdvancedTab.test.tsx`: remove the `onResetComplete`/`onRestartComplete` props from the render call; if there are assertions on those callbacks being called, replace with assertions that the store's `setRestartOverlayOpen` was called (or just that the reset/restart flow completes)

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P3] Create ServerRestartOverlayWrapper
[settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P3] Register ServerRestartOverlay as dialog contribution
tasks.description:
Register the new `ServerRestartOverlayWrapper` (from task 3.4) as a `DialogContribution` in `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/app-layout/model/dialog-contributions.ts`.

**Edit:**

Add the import at the top:

```ts
import { ServerRestartOverlayWrapper } from './wrappers/ServerRestartOverlayWrapper';
```

Append a new entry to the `DIALOG_CONTRIBUTIONS` array:

```ts
{
  id: 'server-restart-overlay',
  component: ServerRestartOverlayWrapper,
  openStateKey: 'restartOverlayOpen',
  priority: 7,
},
```

The `openStateKey: 'restartOverlayOpen'` matches the field added to the panels slice in task 3.2. The `DialogHost` reads this key from the app store to determine whether to render the dialog (see other entries like `tasks` → `'tasksOpen'`).

Acceptance Criteria:
- New entry appended to `DIALOG_CONTRIBUTIONS`
- Import added
- `pnpm typecheck` passes (the `openStateKey` must be a valid key on the panels slice — task 3.2 guarantees this)

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P3] Remove ServerRestartOverlay sibling from SettingsDialog
tasks.description:
Remove the `ServerRestartOverlay` JSX (and its `restartOverlayOpen` local state) from `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx`. After this change, `ServerRestartOverlay` is mounted exclusively via the `DialogHost` registered in task 3.5.

This is a preparatory edit — the full `SettingsDialog` refactor to consume `TabbedDialog` happens in Phase 4 (task 4.1). For now, just delete the lines that wire `restartOverlayOpen`.

**Lines to delete from current `SettingsDialog.tsx`:**

1. Line 23: `import { ServerRestartOverlay } from './ServerRestartOverlay';`
2. Line 43: `const [restartOverlayOpen, setRestartOverlayOpen] = useState(false);`
3. Lines 141-144: the `onResetComplete` / `onRestartComplete` props on `<AdvancedTab>` (after task 3.3 lands, AdvancedTab no longer accepts these props anyway)
4. Lines 171-174: the `<ServerRestartOverlay ... />` JSX block at the end of the dialog

After Phase 3, `<AdvancedTab />` is rendered with no props (matching the parameterless contract from task 3.3).

Acceptance Criteria:
- `SettingsDialog.tsx` no longer imports `ServerRestartOverlay`
- `restartOverlayOpen` local state removed
- `<AdvancedTab />` rendered with no props
- `<ServerRestartOverlay>` JSX removed from the dialog body
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- Manual smoke test: open Settings → Advanced → Reset → confirm `ServerRestartOverlay` still appears (now via `DialogHost`)

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P3] Phase 3 verification gate
tasks.description:
Verify the entire Phase 3 deliverable.

Commands to run:

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/widgets/app-layout
pnpm lint apps/client/src/layers
```

At this point, `SettingsDialog.tsx` still passes `config` and `isLoading` props to `<ServerTab>` (lines 120 in the original). After task 3.1, `ServerTab` no longer accepts those props — so `SettingsDialog.tsx` will fail to typecheck UNLESS task 3.6 also removes the `config={config} isLoading={isLoading}` props from the `<ServerTab>` render. Add this cleanup to task 3.6's edits.

Manual verification:
- [ ] Open Settings dialog → Server tab loads (skeleton briefly visible, then content)
- [ ] Open Settings dialog → Advanced tab → Reset → click confirm → `ServerRestartOverlay` appears (now mounted by `DialogHost`)
- [ ] Open Settings dialog → Advanced tab → Restart → click confirm → `ServerRestartOverlay` appears
- [ ] After dismissing the overlay, the store flag `restartOverlayOpen` is reset to false (verify no double-open on next reset)

Commit message: `refactor(settings): make ServerTab and AdvancedTab self-contained`

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P4] Refactor SettingsDialog to consume TabbedDialog
[settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P4] Verify RemoteAccessAction and extension tabs render
tasks.description:
Verify that `RemoteAccessAction` (the "Remote Access" sidebar item that opens `TunnelDialog`) and any registered extension tabs still render correctly inside the new `<TabbedDialog>`. This is a manual + targeted test verification, not a code edit.

**Manual smoke checklist:**

1. Run `pnpm dev` and open the Settings dialog (gear icon)
2. Verify the sidebar shows: Appearance, Preferences, Status Bar, Server, Tools, Channels, Agents, Advanced — in that order
3. Verify `RemoteAccessAction` ("Remote Access") appears in the sidebar AFTER the Advanced tab (because `TabbedDialog` renders `sidebarExtras` after the tab list per §6.2 notes)
4. Click `RemoteAccessAction` → `TunnelDialog` opens
5. If any extension tabs are registered in `'settings.tabs'` slot, they appear after the built-in tabs
6. Verify extension tab content renders inside its panel (with the Suspense fallback briefly visible if it's lazy)

**If `RemoteAccessAction` placement is wrong**: it currently sits between the Server and Tools tabs in the old SettingsDialog (line 87). After this spec, it sits AFTER all tabs because `sidebarExtras` is rendered after the tab list. **This is an intentional UX change documented in the spec** — confirm with the user during smoke testing if it's acceptable. If not, the alternative is to add `sidebarExtrasPosition?: 'before' | 'between' | 'after'` to `TabbedDialog` (out of scope for this spec — file as a follow-up).

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 8 built-in tabs visible in the sidebar in the correct order
- `RemoteAccessAction` visible in the sidebar (placement may have moved — document current state)
- Extension tabs (if any) render correctly
- No console errors or warnings

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Run full project verification gate
tasks.description:
Run the full repo verification suite to catch any regressions introduced across all five phases.

Commands (run sequentially, each must be green):

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm test -- --run
pnpm lint
pnpm format --check
```

If `pnpm format --check` fails, run `pnpm format` to fix and re-run the check.

Acceptance Criteria:
- `pnpm typecheck` exits 0
- `pnpm test -- --run` exits 0 (all vitest suites pass)
- `pnpm lint` exits 0 (no errors; warnings acceptable per repo convention)
- Prettier formatting clean across all modified files

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Verify file-size targets
tasks.description:
Verify that `SettingsDialog.tsx` and `AgentDialog.tsx` meet the file-size target stated in §1 of the spec: both under 100 lines.

Command:

```bash
wc -l apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx
wc -l apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/AgentDialog.tsx
```

Expected outputs:
- `SettingsDialog.tsx`: ~50 lines (target from §1: "~50 lines")
- `AgentDialog.tsx`: ~60 lines (target from §1: "~60 lines")

If either exceeds 100 lines, investigate why and either:
1. Extract any remaining inline code into a helper, OR
2. Document the deviation from the target in the task close-out (e.g., "AgentDialog is 75 lines because of the necessary `useCallback` wrappers — well within target")

Also run a quick check that no other client file grew unexpectedly:

```bash
find apps/client/src/layers -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.ts' | xargs wc -l | sort -rn | head -20
```

Acceptance Criteria:
- `SettingsDialog.tsx` < 100 lines
- `AgentDialog.tsx` < 100 lines
- No newly-created file exceeds 300 lines (per `.claude/rules/file-size.md`)

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Manual smoke test of ⌘1-⌘9 keyboard shortcuts
tasks.description:
Manually test the new `⌘1`-`⌘9` keyboard shortcuts in both dialogs. This is the only user-visible new feature in the spec.

**SettingsDialog (8 tabs)**:
1. `pnpm dev` and open Settings dialog
2. Verify `⌘1` jumps to Appearance (tab index 0)
3. Verify `⌘2` jumps to Preferences
4. Verify `⌘3` jumps to Status Bar
5. Verify `⌘4` jumps to Server
6. Verify `⌘5` jumps to Tools
7. Verify `⌘6` jumps to Channels
8. Verify `⌘7` jumps to Agents
9. Verify `⌘8` jumps to Advanced
10. Verify `⌘9` does nothing (only 8 built-in tabs; `⌘9` would map to a 9th tab if one existed via extension slot)
11. Verify `⌘0` does nothing
12. Close the dialog. Press `⌘1` outside the dialog. Verify nothing happens (the dialog is closed, so the shortcut handler is detached)
13. Verify the browser does NOT switch tabs (browser ⌘1-⌘9 is captured by the dialog when it has focus)

**AgentDialog (4 tabs)**:
1. Open Agent dialog (from a project with a registered agent)
2. Verify `⌘1` jumps to Identity
3. Verify `⌘2` jumps to Personality
4. Verify `⌘3` jumps to Tools
5. Verify `⌘4` jumps to Channels
6. Verify `⌘5`-`⌘9` do nothing

**Document evidence**: capture a brief note (e.g., a single-line ✓/✗ checklist) in the task close-out. If anything fails, file a follow-up bug and link it.

Acceptance Criteria:
- All numeric shortcuts work as expected in both dialogs
- Shortcuts are scoped to dialog open state
- No conflict with browser tab-switching
- Evidence documented

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Manual smoke test of all tabs and deep-links
tasks.description:
Manually verify every tab in both dialogs renders correctly and that deep-link entry points still work.

**SettingsDialog tab smoke**:
1. Open Settings → click Appearance → verify theme controls render
2. Click Preferences → verify all 9 toggles render (post-2.1 conversion)
3. Click Status Bar → verify the registry list renders with toggles
4. Click Server → verify config rows load (skeleton briefly visible)
5. Click Tools → verify ToolsTab renders
6. Click Channels → verify ChannelsTab renders
7. Click Agents → verify AgentsTab renders (default agent dropdown, runtime cards)
8. Click Advanced → verify Multi-window sync, Background refresh toggles + Logging section + Danger Zone
9. Click Reset in Danger Zone → confirm → verify `ServerRestartOverlay` appears (now mounted via DialogHost)
10. Click Restart in Danger Zone → confirm → verify `ServerRestartOverlay` appears

**AgentDialog tab smoke**:
1. Open Agent dialog (from a project with an agent)
2. Identity → name, role, etc. render
3. Personality → soul/nope content editor renders
4. Tools → tool toggles render
5. Channels → channel bindings render
6. Open Agent dialog from a project WITHOUT an agent → `NoAgentFallback` renders

**Deep-link smoke** (run in DevTools console):
```js
// SettingsDialog deep links
useAppStore.getState().openSettingsToTab('tools');     // Opens Settings → Tools
useAppStore.getState().openSettingsToTab('advanced');  // Opens Settings → Advanced
useAppStore.getState().openSettingsToTab('appearance'); // Opens Settings → Appearance

// AgentDialog deep links
useAppStore.getState().openAgentDialogToTab('personality'); // Opens Agent → Personality
useAppStore.getState().openAgentDialogToTab('tools');       // Opens Agent → Tools
```

Verify that closing the dialog and re-opening with a new `openSettingsToTab(...)` call pre-navigates to the new tab (not the previous one).

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 8 SettingsDialog tabs render their expected content
- All 4 AgentDialog tabs render their expected content
- `NoAgentFallback` renders for agent-less projects
- Deep-link entry points work for both dialogs
- Re-opening with a new initial tab honors the new value (this is the `useDialogTabState` re-sync behavior)
- Evidence documented

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Add Playwright E2E test for ⌘1 shortcut
[settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Document TabbedDialog in contributing/architecture.md
tasks.description:
Add a new section to `contributing/architecture.md` that describes when to reach for `TabbedDialog` vs. using `NavigationLayout` directly. Per §11 of the spec.

**Suggested section** (insert in the appropriate location, likely near existing UI primitive documentation):

```md
### Tabbed dialog primitive (`TabbedDialog`)

`shared/ui/tabbed-dialog.tsx` is the high-level primitive for any sidebar-tabbed dialog in DorkOS. It owns the responsive sidebar, drill-in behavior, animated active-tab pill, deep-link sync via `useDialogTabState`, ⌘1-⌘9 keyboard shortcuts, and extension-slot merging. Both `SettingsDialog` and `AgentDialog` consume it as thin declarative wrappers around a `tabs[]` array.

Reach for `TabbedDialog` when you need:
- A sidebar-navigated dialog with multiple tabs
- Deep-link entry points ("open dialog X to tab Y")
- Responsive mobile drill-in behavior
- Keyboard shortcut tab switching

Reach for `NavigationLayout` directly when you need:
- A bare layout primitive without a `ResponsiveDialog` wrapper (e.g., embedded in a non-modal page)
- Tab content that needs to live outside the parameterless `component: ComponentType` shape
- Custom dialog chrome that doesn't fit the `TabbedDialog` API

For the underlying state-sync hook, see `useDialogTabState` in `shared/model/`.
```

Acceptance Criteria:
- `contributing/architecture.md` updated with the new section
- Cross-link to `useDialogTabState` and `NavigationLayout` included
- No broken markdown links
- Markdown lint clean (if a linter is configured)

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Document useDialogTabState in contributing/state-management.md
tasks.description:
Add `useDialogTabState` as the canonical pattern for dialog deep-link sync in `contributing/state-management.md`. Per §11 of the spec.

**Suggested section** (insert in an appropriate location, e.g., under a "Hooks reference" or "Common patterns" section):

```md
### Dialog deep-link sync — `useDialogTabState`

For any tabbed dialog that needs to be opened to a pre-targeted tab (deep linking), use `useDialogTabState` from `shared/model/`. It implements the React-recommended "adjust state during render" pattern (NOT `useEffect`) and handles re-syncing when the dialog re-opens with a new initial tab.

```ts
const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useDialogTabState({
  open,
  initialTab: store.dialogInitialTab,
  defaultTab: 'first-tab',
});
```

This hook is the single convergence point for tab state across `SettingsDialog`, `AgentDialog`, and any future tabbed dialogs. Do NOT use `useState(initialTab)` plus a separate `useEffect` — that pattern triggers an unnecessary re-render and is what the React 19 docs warn against in "You might not need an effect."

`TabbedDialog` from `shared/ui/` already uses `useDialogTabState` internally — most consumers don't need to import the hook directly.
```

Acceptance Criteria:
- `contributing/state-management.md` updated
- Cross-link to `TabbedDialog` and the React docs included
- Markdown clean

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive] [P6] Add changelog entry for keyboard shortcuts
tasks.description:
Add a brief entry to the next changelog (or wherever DorkOS publishes user-visible release notes) per §11 of the spec.

**Entry content**:

```md
- Settings: ⌘1-⌘9 keyboard shortcuts to switch between tabs
```

Location: find the active changelog file. Common candidates:
- `CHANGELOG.md` at repo root
- `apps/site/content/changelog/...mdx` (Fumadocs)
- `apps/client/CHANGELOG.md`

If none of these exist or the project uses an automated changelog tool (e.g., `changeset`, `changelog:backfill` skill), follow that workflow instead. Use the `writing-changelogs` skill if available.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Changelog entry added (or skipped with a documented reason if the repo uses an automated tool that handles this on release)
- Entry uses the exact wording from the spec
